verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is primarily a secreted cytokine that circulates in a latent complex (LLC/SLC) and binds TGF-β receptors transiently — that interaction is reversible and does not survive washing. However, TGFB1 is also produced as a membrane-anchored latent form: the large latent complex can be tethered to the cell surface via LRRC32 (GARP) or LLTBP on Treg and other cell types, forming a wash-resistant, stable surface complex. In this context, the TGFB1 protein body is genuinely accessible from the extracellular face. This surface form is cell-type and state restricted (Tregs, platelets, certain stromal cells), qualifying as contextual rather than universally yes.
